document_id: 2016 d403tc partnership tax credit summary traditional

findings.description: The PDF has a two-column layout for lines 1-16 where descriptions are on the left and amount fields with line numbers are on the right side. The HTML converts this into a single-column table format, fundamentally changing the visual layout.
findings.evidence: PDF shows a form layout with line descriptions on the left and numbered amount boxes on the right in a side-by-side arrangement. HTML renders this as a table with rows containing 'Line Number', 'Description', and 'Amount' columns.

findings.description: The HTML renders the form fields as a structured table with explicit column headers (Line Number, Description, Amount) which do not exist in the PDF. The PDF uses a form-style layout with fill-in boxes.
findings.evidence: PDF has no table headers - it's a form with numbered lines and blank entry fields. HTML adds 'Line Number', 'Description', and 'Amount' column headers.

findings.description: The NCDOR logo in the PDF header is replaced with plain text 'NCDOR' in the HTML version.
findings.evidence: PDF shows the NCDOR stylized logo with 'Web 7-16' underneath. HTML shows plain text 'NCDOR' with 'Web' and '7-16' below.

findings.description: The form title 'D-403TC' and '2016 Partnership Tax Credit Summary' appear in different styling and positioning in HTML versus PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF shows the title in a bordered header section with large bold text next to the logo. HTML shows it as plain text headings without the bordered section.

findings.description: Part headers (Part 2, Part 3, Part 4) in the PDF are visually distinct section dividers within the form. In the HTML they appear as rows within the table.
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'Part 2. Tax Credits Subject to 50% of Tax Limit' as a bold section header spanning the form width. HTML embeds these as table rows.

findings.description: The reading order is preserved - all content items appear in the same sequence in both versions.
findings.evidence: Lines 1-16 and all part headers appear in the same order in both PDF and HTML.

findings.description: Heading hierarchy is broken. 'NCDOR' is rendered as h1 (the document title should be D-403TC / 2016 Partnership Tax Credit Summary), and the actual form title is h2. Additionally, the heading hierarchy skips from the document structure perspective.
findings.evidence:
HTML has

NCDOR

followed by

D-403TC
2016 Partnership Tax Credit Summary

. The PDF shows 'D-403TC 2016 Partnership Tax Credit Summary' as the document title with NCDOR being the agency logo/branding.

findings.description: Part headings (Part 2, Part 3, Part 4) are embedded within table rows rather than being proper headings, losing the document's structural hierarchy.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, 'Part 2. Tax Credits Subject to 50% of Tax Limit', 'Part 3. Total of Credits Applied to Current Year', and 'Part 4.' are section headings. In the HTML they appear as table rows with no heading tags.

findings.description: The HTML merges what are structurally separate sections/tables in the PDF into a single large table. The PDF has distinct parts (Part 1 lines 1-10, Part 2 line 11, Part 3 line 12, Part 4 lines 13-16) which are visually and structurally separate sections, but the HTML combines them all into one table.
findings.evidence: PDF shows Part 1 (lines 1-10), Part 2 (line 11), Part 3 (line 12), and Part 4 (lines 13-16) as distinct sections. HTML has one continuous table with Part headers as rows within the table.

findings.description: The HTML contains a nested <main> element inside another <main> element, causing structural duplication of the main content wrapper.

Alt text for the barcode image says 'Barcode 7110506016' which appears to be a reasonable interpretation of the barcode visible in the PDF, though the exact number is difficult to verify with certainty.
findings.evidence: HTML: alt='Barcode 7110506016'. PDF shows a barcode with number 7110506016 visible.

findings.description: The Part 1 heading is not explicitly present in the HTML. The PDF implies Part 1 for lines 1-10 (the form structure references 'Part 1, Line 1 through Line 4' in Part 4's instructions).
findings.evidence: PDF Part 4 instructions reference 'a tax credit on Part 1, Line 1 through Line 4'. No 'Part 1' heading appears in the HTML.
